__schema48.title: Discriminator
__schema48.description: When request bodies or response payloads may be one of a number of different schemas, a discriminator object can be used to aid in serialization, deserialization, and validation.

__schema49.description: The name of the property in the payload that will hold the discriminator value.

__schema58.description: The prefix to be used for the name.

__schema75.title: format
__schema75.description: Semantic format hint for string values. Common formats: date-time, email, uuid, uri, hostname, ipv4, byte, binary.

__schema76.title: Pattern

__schema85.title: format
__schema85.description: Number precision format. Use 'float' for 32-bit single-precision or 'double' (default) for 64-bit double-precision IEEE 754.

__schema86.title: multipleOf
__schema86.description: Require the number to be a multiple of this value.

__schema88.title: minimum
__schema88.description: Inclusive lower bound for the number.

__schema90.title: maximum
__schema90.description: Inclusive upper bound for the number.

__schema92.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema92.description: When true, the minimum value is exclusive (value must be > minimum).

__schema94.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema94.description: When true, the maximum value is exclusive (value must be < maximum).

__schema794.title: name
__schema794.description: The name of the query parameter. Case-sensitive.

__schema796.title: description
__schema796.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema801.description: Allows empty values (?param=). Deprecated in favor of schema-level validation.
__schema801.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema804.title: explode
__schema804.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema805.description: When true, allows reserved characters without percent-encoding.
__schema805.title: allowReserved

__schema808.title: Schema Object
__schema808.description: A Schema Object defines the shape of a JSON value.

__schema809.title: String Object
__schema809.description: A String Object defines the shape of a string value.

__schema810.title: title
__schema810.description: A title for the schema, used for documentation.

__schema811.title: description
__schema811.description: A description of the schema. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema812.title: default
__schema812.description: The default value for this schema.

__schema813.title: example
__schema813.description: An example value for this schema.

__schema816.title: enum
__schema816.description: An array of valid values for this schema.

__schema818.title: const
__schema818.description: A constant value that this schema must match.

__schema820.title: Discriminator
__schema820.description: When request bodies or response payloads may be one of a number of different schemas, a discriminator object can be used to aid in serialization, deserialization, and validation.

__schema821.title: propertyName
__schema821.description: The name of the property in the payload that will hold the discriminator value.

__schema822.title: mapping
__schema822.description: An object to hold mappings between payload values and schema names or references.

__schema825.title: defaultMapping
__schema825.description: The default mapping to use when no matching discriminator value is found.

__schema828.title: XML
__schema828.description: A metadata object that allows for more fine-tuned XML model definitions.

__schema829.title: name
__schema829.description: Replaces the name of the element/attribute used for the described schema property.

__schema830.title: namespace
__schema830.description: The URI of the namespace definition.

__schema831.title: prefix
__schema831.description: The prefix to be used for the name.

__schema832.description: Declares whether the property definition translates to an attribute instead of an element.
__schema832.title: attribute

__schema834.description: May be used only for an array definition. Signifies whether the array is wrapped or not.
__schema834.title: wrapped

__schema838.title: ExternalDocumentation
__schema838.description: Allows referencing an external resource for extended documentation.

__schema839.title: description
__schema839.description: A short description of the target documentation.

__schema840.title: url
__schema840.description: The URL for the target documentation.

__schema842.title: readOnly
__schema842.description: When true, the property is only returned in responses, not accepted in requests.

__schema843.title: writeOnly
__schema843.description: When true, the property is only accepted in requests, not returned in responses.

__schema844.title: deprecated
__schema844.description: When true, indicates this schema is deprecated and should be avoided.

__schema847.title: Pattern
__schema847.description: A regular expression pattern the string must match.

__schema854.title: contentMediaType
__schema854.description: Media type of the encoded string content.

__schema857.title: Number Object
__schema857.description: A Number Object defines the shape of a number value.

__schema860.title: multipleOf
__schema860.description: Require the number to be a multiple of this value.

__schema862.title: minimum
__schema862.description: Inclusive lower bound for the number.

__schema864.title: maximum
__schema864.description: Inclusive upper bound for the number.

__schema866.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema866.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema868.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema868.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema870.title: Integer Object
__schema870.description: An Integer Object defines the shape of an integer value.

__schema873.title: multipleOf
__schema873.description: Require the integer to be a multiple of this value.

__schema875.title: minimum
__schema875.description: Inclusive lower bound for the integer.

__schema877.title: maximum
__schema877.description: Inclusive upper bound for the integer.

__schema879.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema879.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema881.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema881.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema883.title: Boolean Object
__schema883.description: A Boolean Object defines the shape of a boolean value.

__schema885.title: Null Object
__schema885.description: A Null Object defines the shape of a null value.

__schema887.title: Array Object
__schema887.description: An Array Object defines the shape of an array value.

__schema891.title: prefixItems
__schema891.description: Tuple validation: schemas for items by index (array position).

__schema893.title: contains
__schema893.description: Require the array to contain at least one item matching this subschema.

__schema905.title: Object Object
__schema905.description: An Object Object defines the shape of an object value.

__schema929.title: Composition Schema
__schema929.description: A schema using composition keywords (allOf, anyOf, oneOf, not, if/then/else) without a type constraint.

__schema930.title: allOf
__schema930.description: Require the instance to validate against all subschemas.

__schema932.title: anyOf
__schema932.description: Require the instance to validate against at least one subschema.

__schema934.title: oneOf
__schema934.description: Require the instance to validate against exactly one subschema.

__schema936.title: not
__schema936.description: Require the instance to NOT validate against the given subschema.

__schema938.title: if
__schema938.description: Conditional validation: if the instance matches this schema, apply then; otherwise apply else.

__schema942.title: else
__schema942.description: Subschema applied when if does not match.

__schema945.title: examples
__schema945.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema952.title: externalValue
__schema952.description: A URL that points to the literal example.

__schema953.title: dataValue
__schema953.description: The data value of the example before serialization. Mutually exclusive with value and externalValue.

__schema954.title: serializedValue
__schema954.description: The serialized representation of the example. Mutually exclusive with value and externalValue.

__schema956.title: content
__schema956.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema958.title: MediaType
__schema958.description: Each Media Type Object provides schema and examples for the media type identified by its key.

__schema966.title: Encoding
__schema966.description: A single encoding definition applied to a single schema property.

__schema967.title: contentType
__schema967.description: The Content-Type for encoding a specific property.

__schema970.title: Header
__schema970.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema971.title: Header
__schema971.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema972.title: description
__schema972.description: A brief description of the parameter.

__schema973.description: Determines whether this parameter is mandatory.
__schema973.title: required

__schema975.description: Specifies that a parameter is deprecated.
__schema975.title: deprecated

__schema977.description: Sets the ability to pass empty-valued parameters.
__schema977.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema979.title: style
__schema979.description: Describes how the parameter value will be serialized.

__schema1003.title: itemSchema
__schema1003.description: Schema for individual items in streaming responses.

__schema1004.title: itemEncoding
__schema1004.description: Encoding for individual items in streaming responses.

__schema1009.title: name
__schema1009.description: The name of the path parameter. Must match a template expression in the path.

__schema1011.title: description
__schema1011.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema1012.description: Path parameters must always be required.
__schema1012.title: required

__schema1016.title: explode
__schema1016.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema1019.title: examples
__schema1019.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema1021.title: content
__schema1021.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema1025.title: name
__schema1025.description: The name of the header. Case-insensitive.

__schema1027.title: description
__schema1027.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema1032.description: Serialization style. Only 'simple' is valid for headers.

__schema1033.title: explode
__schema1033.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema1036.title: examples
__schema1036.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema1038.title: content
__schema1038.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema1044.title: description
__schema1044.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema1049.description: Serialization style. Only 'form' is valid for cookies.

__schema1050.title: explode
__schema1050.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema1053.title: examples
__schema1053.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema1055.title: content
__schema1055.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema1070.title: Response
__schema1070.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema1071.title: Response
__schema1071.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema1079.title: Link
__schema1079.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema1080.title: Link
__schema1080.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema1081.title: operationId
__schema1081.description: The name of an existing, resolvable OAS operation, as defined with a unique operationId.

__schema1082.title: operationRef
__schema1082.description: A relative or absolute URI reference to an OAS operation.

__schema1083.title: parameters
__schema1083.description: A map representing parameters to pass to an operation as specified with operationId or identified via operationRef.

__schema1086.title: requestBody
__schema1086.description: A literal value or {expression} to use as a request body when calling the target operation.

__schema1089.title: Server
__schema1089.description: An object representing a Server. OpenAPI 3.2 adds 'name' for identification.

__schema1090.title: url
__schema1090.description: A URL to the target host. Supports server variables in {braces}. May be relative.

__schema1091.title: description
__schema1091.description: An optional description of the server.

__schema1092.title: variables
__schema1092.description: A map of server variables for URL template substitution.

__schema1094.title: ServerVariable
__schema1094.description: A server variable for URL template substitution. Use {variableName} in the server URL.

__schema1095.title: enum
__schema1095.description: An enumeration of valid string values for this variable.

__schema1097.title: default
__schema1097.description: The default value for substitution if not supplied.

__schema1098.title: description
__schema1098.description: An optional description for the server variable.

__schema1100.title: name
__schema1100.description: A unique name to identify the server.

__schema1108.description: Marks this operation as deprecated. Defaults to false.

__schema1109.title: security
__schema1109.description: Security requirements for this operation. Overrides root-level security.

__schema1110.title: SecurityRequirement
__schema1110.description: Lists the required security schemes for this operation.

__schema1125.title: additionalOperations
__schema1125.description: Custom HTTP methods beyond the standard ones.

__schema1131.title: SecurityScheme
__schema1131.description: Defines a security scheme that can be used by the operations.

__schema1134.title: name
__schema1134.description: The name of the header, query, or cookie parameter.

__schema1135.description: Location of the API key: 'header' (most common), 'query', or 'cookie'.

__schema1136.title: description
__schema1136.description: A description of the security scheme.

__schema1138.title: HttpSecurityScheme
__schema1138.description: HTTP authentication security scheme (Basic, Bearer, etc.).

__schema1141.title: bearerFormat
__schema1141.description: Hint for bearer token format. Only applicable when scheme='bearer'.

__schema1142.title: description
__schema1142.description: A description of the security scheme.

__schema1151.title: flows
__schema1151.description: Allows configuration of the supported OAuth Flows.

__schema1153.title: OAuthFlow
__schema1153.description: Configuration details for a supported OAuth Flow.

__schema1154.title: authorizationUrl
__schema1154.description: The authorization URL to be used for this flow.

__schema1155.title: tokenUrl
__schema1155.description: The token URL to be used for this flow.

__schema1156.title: refreshUrl
__schema1156.description: The URL to be used for obtaining refresh tokens.

__schema1157.title: scopes
__schema1157.description: The available scopes for the OAuth2 security scheme.

__schema1164.title: device
__schema1164.description: Configuration for the Device Authorization Grant flow.

__schema1171.title: openIdConnectUrl
__schema1171.description: OpenID Connect URL to discover OAuth2 configuration values.

additionalProperties.title: Callback
additionalProperties.description: A map of possible out-of-band callbacks related to the parent operation.

__schema1184.title: Callback
__schema1184.description: A map of possible out-of-band callbacks related to the parent operation.

__schema1264.description: A short description of the target documentation.
__schema1264.title: description

__schema1266.title: url
__schema1266.description: REQUIRED. The URL for the target documentation.
